response.justification: Maria Rodriguez brings genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and has touched high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby, but the core technical requirements of this role are largely unmet. C++ experience is limited to a 6-month stint writing components for NI LabWindows/CVI and a bachelor thesis project; there is no Rust experience at all. The primary commercial stack for the last eight-plus years is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, none of which are relevant to a C++-first routing tile pipeline. Her GIS background is centered on data collection and NGO mapping tools, not routing graph construction, tile generation, or navigation accuracy — the actual domain of this role.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development (GISCollective, Green Map GIS app, FOSS4G speaker) demonstrates domain adjacency
Dunnhumby role shows experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed micro-services handling millions of requests per second
Long Linux background and open-source development culture align with the server-side infrastructure environment
concerns: No meaningful C++ or Rust experience in recent history; C++ appears only in a 6-month job from 2014–2015 and a university thesis
Zero routing tile pipeline, navigation guidance, or map data transformation experience — the core domain of the role
GIS work is data-collection and NGO-mapping oriented, not optimized routing graph or tile-generation pipelines at production scale
